title: Postgres Enterprise Manager 10.5.0 release notes
navTitle: Version 10.5.0
originalFilePath: product_docs/docs/pem/10/pem_rel_notes/src/pem_10.5.0_rel_notes.yml

Released: 22 June 2026


!!!Note PEM sizing and scalability
PEM 10.5 includes performance enhancements that have resulted in changes to our [sizing guidance](/pem/latest/considerations/sizing.mdx).
We recommend you review this to ensure your system is right-sized for PEM 10.5
!!!

!!!Note Changes to CLI option parsing
PEM 10.5 features more robust option parsing for the `pemworker` CLI and for the `configure_pem_server.sh`.
Some combinations of options that were previously accepted may no longer be accepted, so please review any scripts or automations that use these CLIs when upgrading.
!!!

New features, enhancements, bug fixes, and other changes in Postgres Enterprise Manager 10.5.0 include the following:

## Highlights

- Improved UX for assigning and creating profiles
- Multiple enhancements to the new reporting capabilities including the ability to run custom reports against the PEM server itself
- Significantly improved alert evaluation performance, particularly in systems with more than one alert thread

## Enhancements

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Introduced a Server Downtime Report tracking availability with planned versus unplanned downtime classification.</summary><hr/><p>Introduced a Server Downtime Report that tracks server availability by monitoring heartbeat gaps, providing uptime/downtime analysis with planned versus unplanned downtime classification using blackout periods.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added probe data and history REST API endpoints (v17) for multiple objects per call.</summary><hr/><p>Added probe data and history REST API endpoints (v17) that expose monitoring data from the <code>pemdata</code> and <code>pemhistory</code> tables for multiple objects in a single call, supporting both numeric probe ID and internal name addressing.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Introduced Cluster Topology Discovery and Visualization with interactive SVG diagrams for replication clusters.</summary><hr/><p>Introduced Cluster Topology Discovery and Visualization, which automatically discovers PostgreSQL replication clusters (physical streaming replication, Patroni, PGD, EFM) and renders interactive SVG topology diagrams in the PEM browser tree, with support for cascading replication, lag display, and role badges.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added a Profile Assignments page to view and change profile assignments inline or in bulk.</summary><hr/><p>Added a new Profile Assignments page (Management &gt; Monitoring &gt; Profile Assignments) that displays all servers and agents in a tree view grouped by server group and cluster, allowing authorized users to view and change profile assignments inline or in bulk via multi-select.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Implemented team-based RBAC filtering for report templates with row-level security policies.</summary><hr/><p>Implemented team-based RBAC filtering for report templates, allowing administrators to designate reports as PEM-only (running on the PEM database) with row-level security policies that enforce team-based visibility.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added repeating section support to report templates, iterating over group query rows.</summary><hr/><p>Added repeating section support to report templates, enabling report sections to iterate over rows from a group query with per-row parameter substitution to produce structured multi-instance output within a single section.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added profile import/export endpoints for replicating monitoring profiles across PEM installations.</summary><hr/><p>Added profile import/export endpoints that enable users to export monitoring profiles as JSON files and import them into other PEM installations for easy configuration replication across environments.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added the ability to clone an existing profile via the Add Profile dialog.</summary><hr/><p>Added the ability to clone an existing profile via the Add Profile dialog, allowing administrators to create new profiles based on existing configurations without manual reconfiguration.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added application-layer authorization gates requiring management roles for PEM REST API write endpoints.</summary><hr/><p>Added application-layer authorization gates for PEM REST API write endpoints, requiring callers to hold the relevant PEM management role (for example, asset management or alert management) for write operations.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Introduced search-by-name capability for monitored objects in REST API v17.</summary><hr/><p>Introduced search-by-name capability for API v17, enabling users to search for monitored objects (agents, servers, databases) by name through the REST API.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added <code>server_id</code> and <code>agent_id</code> to the System Configuration Report's delayed alerts section.</summary><hr/><p>Enhanced the System Configuration Report to include <code>server_id</code> and <code>agent_id</code> in the delayed alerts section, making it easier to identify which server or agent is affected by delayed alert processing.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added multi-server and multi-agent selection to the Alert History Report modal.</summary><hr/><p>Added support for selecting multiple servers and agents in the Alert History Report modal, enabling users to generate reports spanning multiple monitored targets in a single execution.</p>
</details></td><td>57611</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added database name display to the report history table and executed report header.</summary><hr/><p>Added database name display to the report history table and executed report header, giving users clear context about which database a report was executed against.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added the <code>is_pem_report</code> flag so the Core Usage Report runs on the PEM database.</summary><hr/><p>Added the <code>is_pem_report</code> flag to the Core Usage Report, ensuring it runs on the PEM database and appears in the appropriate report category.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Re-introduced the &quot;Show Acknowledged Alerts&quot; setting on the alert dashboard.</summary><hr/><p>Re-introduced the &quot;Show Acknowledged Alerts&quot; setting on the alert dashboard, allowing users to toggle visibility of acknowledged alerts in the monitoring view.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added a navigation link from Manage Profiles to Profile Assignments.</summary><hr/><p>Added a navigation link from Manage Profiles to Profile Assignments, improving workflow efficiency when administrators need to switch between profile configuration and assignment tasks.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added webhook test connection support with OAuth2 authentication.</summary><hr/><p>Added webhook test connection support with OAuth2 authentication, enabling users to verify webhook endpoint connectivity when OAuth2 credentials are configured.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added configuration options to specify the TLS version for agent WebClient operations.</summary><hr/><p>Added configuration options to specify the TLS version for different WebClient operations in the agent, allowing administrators to enforce specific TLS versions for enhanced security compliance.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Re-introduced the <code>ENABLE_DATA_ACCESS_TOOLS</code> and <code>ENABLE_DEBUGGER</code> settings dropped in PEM 10.0.</summary><hr/><p>Re-introduced the <code>ENABLE_DATA_ACCESS_TOOLS</code> and <code>ENABLE_DEBUGGER</code> configuration settings (default <code>True</code>) that were dropped in the PEM 10.0 release.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Batched <code>session_info</code> CPU sampling across backends to reduce per-session collection overhead.</summary><hr/><p>Batched <code>session_info</code> CPU sampling across all backends, reducing the overhead of per-session CPU usage collection on systems with many active connections.</p>
</details></td><td>59499</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Optimized <code>probe_target_view</code> SQL using CTEs and removed unnecessary joins to improve performance.</summary><hr/><p>Optimized the SQL of <code>probe_target_view</code> to use CTEs and removed unnecessary joins for probe target levels below database, improving query performance.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Optimized the purge job by combining age-based and obsolete-object purging into one function.</summary><hr/><p>Optimized the purge job by combining age-based and obsolete-object purging into a single function, reducing the number of database scans required for data cleanup.</p>
</details></td><td>56990</td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Security Fixes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-7815) in the Maintenance Tool options.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-7815) in the Maintenance Tool options, where unsafe values for <code>vacuum_index_cleanup</code>, <code>vacuum_parallel</code>, and <code>buffer_usage_limit</code> were rendered directly into SQL templates; server-side allow-list validation and <code>qtIdent</code> escaping for tablespace identifiers were added.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an OS command injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-7816) in the Import/Export query export.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an OS command injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-7816) in the Import/Export query export, via unsanitized query content and format options passed to the <code>psql</code> template; allow-list validation and a balanced-parenthesis query parser were added.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a pickle deserialization RCE vulnerability (CVE-2026-7818) in the session manager.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a pickle deserialization remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2026-7818) in the session manager, where a crafted session file could execute arbitrary code under the PEM process; session files are now HMAC-verified with <code>hmac.compare_digest</code> before deserialization.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a symlink path traversal vulnerability (CVE-2026-7819) in the File Manager.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a symlink path traversal vulnerability (CVE-2026-7819) in the File Manager that allowed an authenticated user to write files outside their storage directory; <code>check_access_permission</code> now uses <code>os.path.realpath</code> and file uploads use <code>O_NOFOLLOW</code>.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-12044) in dialog COMMENT statements and pgstattuple.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-12044) across dialog templates that rendered <code>COMMENT ON ... IS '&lt;description&gt;'</code> statements, by switching to the <code>qtLiteral</code> escape filter and hardening <code>qtLiteral</code> to raise rather than silently return raw values when <code>conn</code> is falsy; also closed the pgstattuple/pgstatindex sink whereby a low-privilege user naming a table or index with an apostrophe-laden identifier caused SQL injection under the superuser viewer's role. Reported by Jasser Chebbi.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an AI Assistant read-only transaction bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-12045) enabling arbitrary SQL execution.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an AI Assistant read-only transaction bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-12045) that allowed prompt-injected multi-statement payloads to commit out of the <code>READ ONLY</code> wrapper and execute arbitrary SQL, chaining to remote code execution via <code>COPY ... TO PROGRAM</code> on a superuser connection. Reported by Isaac Chen.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed two SQL Editor endpoints missing authentication (CVE-2026-12046) that exposed a deserialization sink.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed two SQL Editor endpoints (<code>close</code> and <code>update_connection</code>) that were missing the <code>@pga_login_required</code> decorator (CVE-2026-12046), making them reachable without authentication in server mode and exposing a pickle deserialization sink. Reported by Fernando Bortotti.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a stored XSS vulnerability (CVE-2026-12048) via server error text and Explain content.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability (CVE-2026-12048) via PostgreSQL server error text and Explain plan-node content passed through <code>html-react-parser</code> without sanitization across notifier toasts, form errors, modal alerts, and the Explain visualizer, whereby same-origin JavaScript could read saved server connection credentials and issue arbitrary SQL against every connected server. Reported by Fernando Bortotti.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-12050) in the named restore point endpoint.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-12050) in the named restore point endpoint, where the user-supplied restore point name was interpolated into SQL via <code>str.format()</code> instead of being passed as a bound parameter; the fix was applied to both the pgAdmin <code>ServerNode</code> and the PEM <code>PemServerNode</code> implementations. Reported by Geo.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a stored XSS vulnerability (CVE-2026-7814) via <code>innerHTML</code> in tree labels and Explain visualizer.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability (CVE-2026-7814) via <code>innerHTML</code> in the browser tree node labels and the Explain visualizer; all <code>innerHTML</code> assignments were replaced with <code>textContent</code>.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an account lockout bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-7820) via the Flask-Security <code>/login</code> endpoint.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an account lockout bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-7820), where an attacker who hit the lockout threshold on <code>/authenticate/login</code> could continue brute-forcing via the Flask-Security <code>/login</code> endpoint; <code>User.is_active</code> and <code>User.is_locked</code> now gate both authentication paths.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an HTML injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-12047) in the cloud deployment module.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an HTML injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-12047) in the cloud deployment module (RDS, Azure, Google), where SDK exception text was forwarded to the browser unsanitized and rendered through <code>html-react-parser</code> in the Cloud Wizard. Reported by Fernando Bortotti.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an open redirect vulnerability (CVE-2026-12049) in the multi-factor authentication flow.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an open redirect vulnerability (CVE-2026-12049) in the multi-factor authentication flow, via an unvalidated 'next' parameter that allowed a crafted link to redirect an authenticated victim to an attacker-controlled host. Reported by Mai Phạm Hiền.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated dompurify to 3.4.8 to fix BDSA-2026-13267.</summary><hr/><p>Updated dompurify to 3.4.8 to fix BDSA-2026-13267.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Upgraded HTTPD to version 2.4.67 for the PEM server installer.</summary><hr/><p>Upgraded HTTPD to version 2.4.67 for the PEM server installer, incorporating the latest security fixes and stability improvements.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Upgraded bundled PostgreSQL to version 17.10-1 for the PEM server installer on Windows.</summary><hr/><p>Upgraded bundled PostgreSQL to version 17.10-1 for the PEM server installer on Windows, incorporating the latest security fixes and stability improvements.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Closed 18 Dependabot security alerts with minimal pgAdmin-origin code churn.</summary><hr/><p>Closed 18 Dependabot security alerts with minimal pgAdmin-origin code churn.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Changes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Reworked alert blackouts into first-class PEM objects with REST API v17 and server-side processing.</summary><hr/><p>Reworked alert blackouts from job-based artifacts into first-class PEM objects with a normalized schema, REST API v17 endpoints, an audit trail, a manual enable/disable toggle, and a server-side <code>process_blackouts()</code> system job that replaces the legacy agent-side blackout management.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Migrated the Core Usage Report to the custom report template framework.</summary><hr/><p>Migrated the Core Usage Report to the custom report template framework, enabling scheduled execution, API access, and consistent management alongside other custom reports.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Migrated the System Configuration Report to the custom report template framework.</summary><hr/><p>Migrated the System Configuration Report to the custom report template framework, consolidating all system reports under the unified template-based architecture.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Refactored alert dispatch into a queue-driven model enabling concurrent workers without contention.</summary><hr/><p>Refactored alert dispatch from per-cycle template scanning into a queue-driven model using <code>pem.alert_queue</code> with <code>FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED</code> concurrency, enabling concurrent alert workers without contention and deferring queued alerts via <code>due_at</code> rather than dropping them during blackouts.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Refreshed the <code>probe_target_view</code> materialized view concurrently to eliminate table locks during refresh.</summary><hr/><p>Modified the <code>pem.refresh_stale_probe_view</code> function to refresh the <code>probe_target_view</code> materialized view concurrently, eliminating table locks during the refresh job and improving system responsiveness under load.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Upgraded SNMP++ to version 3.6.6 with upstream bug fixes and improvements.</summary><hr/><p>Upgraded SNMP++ to version 3.6.6, incorporating upstream bug fixes and improvements to the SNMP notification subsystem.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Hardened agent connection, spooler, and async-query paths to eliminate hangs and silent failures.</summary><hr/><p>Hardened the agent connection, spooler, and async-query paths to eliminate hangs, poisoned-row loops, and silent failure modes, with <code>poll()</code>-driven async query execution that supports timeout and cancel, a shared <code>SpoolerLoop</code> driver, capped spool retries, and typed <code>ErrorKind</code> categories.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Increased the default <code>PEM_DB_POOL_MAX_WAITING</code> from 10 to 30 to reduce pool exhaustion.</summary><hr/><p>Increased the default <code>PEM_DB_POOL_MAX_WAITING</code> from 10 to 30, reducing connection pool exhaustion under concurrent load by allowing more requests to queue before being rejected.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Modernized the command-line argument parsing framework for <code>pemWorker</code>.</summary><hr/><p>Modernized the command-line argument parsing framework for <code>pemWorker</code>, improving maintainability and enabling cleaner addition of new CLI options.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated copyright notices to 2026 across the PEM source files.</summary><hr/><p>Updated copyright notices to 2026 across the PEM source files.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Merged the pgAdmin 9.13 codebase into PEM, bringing the latest features and fixes.</summary><hr/><p>Merged the pgAdmin 9.13 codebase into PEM, bringing the latest pgAdmin features, bug fixes, and improvements to the PEM user interface and core functionality.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated Django (webhook_mtls_app) to 4.2.26 LTS.</summary><hr/><p>Updated Django (webhook_mtls_app) to 4.2.26 LTS.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated urllib3 to 2.7.0.</summary><hr/><p>Updated urllib3 to 2.7.0.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated axios to 1.18.0.</summary><hr/><p>Updated axios to 1.18.0.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated React to 19.2.6.</summary><hr/><p>Updated React to 19.2.6.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated qs to 6.15.2.</summary><hr/><p>Updated qs to 6.15.2.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated lodash to 4.18.1.</summary><hr/><p>Updated lodash to 4.18.1.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated postcss to 8.5.15.</summary><hr/><p>Updated postcss to 8.5.15.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated Authlib to 1.6.12.</summary><hr/><p>Updated Authlib to 1.6.12.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated follow-redirects to 1.16.0.</summary><hr/><p>Updated follow-redirects to 1.16.0.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated form-data to 4.0.6.</summary><hr/><p>Updated form-data to 4.0.6.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Bug Fixes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed the agent using stale cached credentials after rotation by invalidating the ASB cache.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby the agent's Agent Server Binding (ASB) cache was not invalidated when a monitored server connection failed because of invalid credentials, causing the agent to continue using stale cached passwords after credential rotation; the agent now evicts the ASB cache and clears pooler credentials on connection failure, and refuses to fall back to cached plaintext when <code>EDBPEMDecrypt</code> fails.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed <code>create_system_report</code> failing silently on duplicate titles, enabling idempotent upgrade re-runs.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby <code>create_system_report</code> failed silently on a duplicate title instead of raising an exception, enabling idempotent re-runs of upgrade scripts on already-upgraded installations.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a syntax error when editing alert parameter values in the Manage Alerts panel.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a syntax error that occurred when editing parameter values for alerts from the Manage Alerts panel.</p>
</details></td><td>59918</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a typo in <code>purge_snmp_spool</code> that prevented the <code>snmp_spool</code> table from being purged.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a typo in the <code>purge_snmp_spool</code> function that prevented the <code>snmp_spool</code> table from being purged properly, causing accumulated unsent SNMP notifications.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed global-agent server-level alerts being silently filtered out of the alert queue.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby global-agent server-specific alerts (<code>agent_id</code> = 0 or -1) were silently filtered out of the alert queue because the eligibility predicate incorrectly required an <code>agent_server_binding</code> match on <code>agent_id</code>, which caused roughly 66% of auto-created server-level alerts never to fire.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a copy alerts crash and a 10.3.1-to-10.4.1 upgrade failure from double-encoded <code>connection_params</code>.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a copy alerts crash caused by scope-filter tightening that excluded fixtures with non-canonical <code>package_name</code>/<code>agent_id</code> values, and fixed a 10.3.1 to 10.4.1 upgrade failure whereby double-encoded <code>connection_params</code> JSONB strings caused <code>jsonb_each</code> to abort the migration.</p>
</details></td><td>61992</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed server filtering in the Barman dashboard's candle chart.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby it was not possible to filter by server in the Barman dashboard's candle chart, restoring server-level filtering.</p>
</details></td><td>60789</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a duplicate function entry in <code>pemserver.sql</code> and corrected an EFM alert template name.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a duplicate entry for the <code>pem.create_delete_probe_purge_jobstep()</code> function in <code>pemserver.sql</code> and corrected the alert template name &quot;EFM Fewer Than N Nodes Active&quot; in the 10.3.0 upgrade script.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Aligned <code>pem_assign_profile</code> behavior with its documented profile assignment contract.</summary><hr/><p>Aligned <code>pem_assign_profile</code> with its documented contract, fixing inconsistencies between its actual behavior and the expected profile assignment semantics.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed the Performance Diagnostic tool's recovery from disconnected server states and header layout.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby the Performance Diagnostic tool did not recover gracefully from disconnected server states, and corrected the responsive header layout.</p>
</details></td><td>60789</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed dashboard charts not refreshing when navigating contexts via breadcrumb navigation.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby dashboard charts did not refresh when navigating between contexts via the breadcrumb navigation.</p>
</details></td><td>60091, 61637</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed workspace ad-hoc database connections failing from the query tool due to model misuse.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby workspace ad-hoc database connections did not use the PEM Server model correctly, which caused connection failures from the query tool.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed the chart color settings API reading the transaction ID from the wrong source.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby the chart color settings API failed because the transaction ID was read from the prop instead of from dashboard state.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Granted <code>pg_read_server_files</code> and <code>pg_write_server_files</code> roles to <code>pem_agent</code> to resolve file-access errors.</summary><hr/><p>Granted the <code>pg_read_server_files</code> and <code>pg_write_server_files</code> roles to <code>pem_agent</code>, resolving permission errors when the agent needs to access server-side files for monitoring operations.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed the unqualified <code>insert_jobinfolog</code> function causing job logging failures in some contexts.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby the <code>insert_jobinfolog</code> function was not schema-qualified, causing job logging failures in certain execution contexts.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed PEM agent.log output missing newline characters that ran log entries together.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby PEM agent.log output was missing newline characters, causing log entries to run together and making log analysis difficult.</p>
</details></td><td>60923</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Eliminated spurious warning messages when registering a new agent.</summary><hr/><p>Eliminated spurious warning messages displayed when registering a new agent, reducing noise in the registration workflow.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added an in-process retry queue for probe and job unlock operations after transient connection loss.</summary><hr/><p>Added an in-process retry queue for probe and job unlock operations that fail because of transient PEM connection loss, preventing probes from remaining stuck in flight until the next worker restart.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed the <code>purge_deleted_objects_data</code> job not being created for UI-deleted objects.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby the <code>purge_deleted_objects_data</code> job was not created for objects deleted from the UI, and modified the purge function to disable triggers before deleting data from the <code>pemdata</code> schema to prevent copying to <code>pemhistory</code> during purge.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed API documentation and validation for the <code>/agent/job</code> endpoint.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed the API documentation and validation for the <code>/agent/job</code> endpoint, ensuring correct request and response schemas.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed <code>edb-uwsgi</code> generating a core dump during service restart.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby <code>edb-uwsgi</code> generated a core dump during service restart, by correcting the process lifecycle handling.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed PEM upgrade to error out when an upgrade script fails, preventing partial upgrades.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed PEM upgrade so that it now errors out when an upgrade script fails, preventing silent partial upgrades that leave the system in an inconsistent state.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed Nginx to pass the real hostname and HTTPS scheme through to the application.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed the Nginx configuration so that the real hostname and HTTPS scheme are passed through to the uWSGI/Flask application, resolving issues with URL generation and redirect handling behind the reverse proxy.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed the PEM database connection pool to heal after a backend restart.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed the PEM database connection pool so that it heals after a backend restart, preventing <code>NoneType</code> crashes in the web UI by adding pool-level health checks and proper handling of stale connections.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed Performance Diagnostic charts to backfill empty timeline buckets for continuous rendering.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed Performance Diagnostic charts so that empty timeline buckets are backfilled, ensuring continuous chart rendering even when some time periods have no data.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Hardened the probe REST API and fixed the Create Profile dialog's duplicate-name validation error.</summary><hr/><p>Hardened the probe REST API (blocking SQL edits on system probes, rejecting duplicate <code>probe_name</code> on POST, and surfacing unknown IDs on export) and fixed the Create Profile dialog to show an inline validation error on duplicate profile names instead of the raw PostgreSQL unique-constraint error.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed &quot;Show acknowledged alerts&quot; not restoring alerts in the Alert Details chart.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby toggling &quot;Show acknowledged alerts&quot; back to enabled would not restore alerts in the Alert Details chart after all alerts had been acknowledged, because the error state was not cleared when data returned successfully.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed WCAG 2.0 AA contrast issues across the dark, light, and high-contrast themes.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed WCAG 2.0 AA contrast issues across the dark, light, and high-contrast themes, improving accessibility for users with visual impairments.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed editing a custom batch probe's platform not updating the database platform column.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby editing a custom batch probe's platform (for example, changing from *nix to Windows) did not update the platform column in the database, causing the probe not to appear in <code>probe_target_view</code> for the intended agent.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed <code>pem_manage_alert</code> users unable to create alerts via the REST API.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby a user with the <code>pem_manage_alert</code> role (via <code>pem_config_alert</code>) could not create alerts via the REST API because of missing <code>SELECT</code>, <code>INSERT</code>, <code>UPDATE</code>, and <code>DELETE</code> permissions on the <code>pem.webhook_alert_config</code> table.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed <code>--disable-probe</code> and <code>--enable-probe</code> CLI commands failing on database-level probes.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed an issue whereby the <code>--disable-probe</code> and <code>--enable-probe</code> CLI commands failed on database-level probes with a &quot;permission denied for table <code>probe_config_database</code>&quot; error.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
</tbody></table>
